name: Requirements Engineering
description: Requirements engineering turns stakeholder needs into a validated specification: requirements are elicited, analyzed, and classified as functional behavior or non-functional quality attributes; conflicts and ambiguities are negotiated and resolved, the result is validated with stakeholders, and the agreed set is baselined into a specification.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Stakeholder needs"]
    N2["Elicit requirements"]
    N3["Analyze + classify"]
    N4{"Functional or not?"}
    N5["Specify behavior"]
    N6["Specify quality attrs"]
    N7{"Conflicts / ambiguity?"}
    N8["Negotiate + resolve"]
    N9["Validate w/ stakeholders"]
    N10["Baseline specification"]
    N11["Requirements doc"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|functional| N5
    N4 -->|non-functional| N6
    N5 --> N7
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 -->|rework| N3
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

sources.title: Requirements engineering: a roadmap
sources.journal: Proceedings of the Conference on the Future of Software Engineering
